input.initialPrompt: I have some failing tests and want to use hot reload to iterate faster. Can you set it up for me?

data.reasoningText: The user wants to set up hot reload for their .NET test project so they can iterate faster on failing tests. Let me first look at the project files to understand the setup.

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.description: Start dotnet watch test for hot reload

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Start dotnet watch test for hot reload

data.reasoningText:
The user wants hot reload for their test project. `dotnet watch test` is the way to go. Let me start it up for them. I can see the test project uses MSTest with .NET 8.0.

Looking at the tests, the express delivery test expects 15.00 but the method returns 10.00, so that test will fail. But the user just wants hot reload set up, not the fix.

Let me start `dotnet watch test` in async mode so it stays running and they can iterate.

arguments.description: Start dotnet watch test for hot reload

arguments.description: Touch file to trigger watch rebuild

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Touch file to trigger watch rebuild

data.reasoningText:
The watch didn't pick up the change. It might be waiting for a file-change event but the file was edited in a way that didn't trigger it. Let me try touching the file or restarting the watch.

Actually looking at the error message more carefully: "Fix the error to continue or press Ctrl+C to exit." - it's watching for file changes. The edit might have happened before the watch started monitoring. Let me touch the file to trigger a re-evaluation.
